title: Sequences, Recurrences, and Discrete Change · Mixed Practice Return
summary: Forecast savings, inventory, and staged population processes. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a discrete recurrence forecast after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent explicit and recursive sequences and state valid index domains.
Model constant differences and constant ratios with arithmetic and geometric sequences.
Calculate finite sums and explain their accumulated quantity and units.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

stimulus.title: Discrete Stability case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math intermediate sequences discrete. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A small one-step error remains small forever.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inspect long-run behavior and sensitivity of discrete models. Artifact under review: a discrete recurrence forecast.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Discrete Stability applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
